name: @radzor/prompt-template
description: Manage and render prompt templates with variable interpolation, conditional sections, and few-shot example injection. Supports template registration, validation, and composable prompt construction for LLM workflows.

inputs.name: delimiters
inputs.description: Custom delimiters for variable interpolation. Defaults to {{ and }}.

inputs.name: strictMode
inputs.description: When true, rendering throws if any variables are unresolved. When false, unresolved variables remain as-is.

outputs.name: renderedPrompt
outputs.description: The fully rendered prompt string with all variables resolved and examples injected.

fields.name: templateName
fields.description: Name of the template that was rendered.

fields.name: variablesUsed
fields.description: List of variable names that were resolved.

actions.name: render

params.name: name
params.description: Name of a registered template to render.

params.name: variables
params.description: Key-value map of variables to substitute.

params.name: examples
params.description: Few-shot examples to inject at the {{examples}} placeholder.

actions.description: Render a registered template with the given variables and optional few-shot examples.

actions.name: list
actions.description: List all registered templates with their variable names and defaults.

actions.name: validate

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the template to validate.

params.name: variables
params.description: Variables to check against the template.

actions.description: Validate a template: check for unresolved variables, unclosed delimiters, and missing required variables.

llm.constraints: Pure computation — no network calls, no API keys required. Runs in any JavaScript/Python environment. Template syntax uses {{ }} delimiters by default.
